members.name: EnsureControlVisible
members.summary: Ensures the given [param control] is visible (must be a direct or indirect child of the ScrollContainer). Used by [member follow_focus]. [b]Note:[/b] This will not work on a node that was just added during the same frame. If you want to scroll to a newly added child, you must wait until the next frame using [signal SceneTree.process_frame]: [codeblock] add_child(child_node) await get_tree().process_frame ensure_control_visible(child_node) [/codeblock]

members.name: GetHScrollBar
members.summary: Returns the horizontal scrollbar [HScrollBar] of this [ScrollContainer]. [b]Warning:[/b] This is a required internal node, removing and freeing it may cause a crash. If you wish to disable or hide a scrollbar, you can use [member horizontal_scroll_mode].

members.name: GetVScrollBar
members.summary: Returns the vertical scrollbar [VScrollBar] of this [ScrollContainer]. [b]Warning:[/b] This is a required internal node, removing and freeing it may cause a crash. If you wish to disable or hide a scrollbar, you can use [member vertical_scroll_mode].

members.name: DrawFocusBorder
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], [theme_item focus] is drawn when the ScrollContainer or one of its descendant nodes is focused.

members.name: FollowFocus
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the ScrollContainer will automatically scroll to focused children (including indirect children) to make sure they are fully visible.

members.name: HorizontalScrollMode
members.summary: Controls whether horizontal scrollbar can be used and when it should be visible.

members.name: ScrollDeadzone
members.summary: Deadzone for touch scrolling. Lower deadzone makes the scrolling more sensitive.

members.name: ScrollHintMode
members.summary: The way which scroll hints (indicators that show that the content can still be scrolled in a certain direction) will be shown. [b]Note:[/b] Hints won't be shown if the content can be scrolled both vertically and horizontally.

members.name: ScrollHorizontal
members.summary: The current horizontal scroll value. [b]Note:[/b] If you are setting this value in the [method Node._ready] function or earlier, it needs to be wrapped with [method Object.set_deferred], since scroll bar's [member Range.max_value] is not initialized yet. [codeblock] func _ready(): set_deferred("scroll_horizontal", 600) [/codeblock]

members.name: ScrollHorizontalByDefault
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the mouse wheel scrolls the view horizontally, and holding [kbd]Shift[/kbd] scrolls vertically. If [code]false[/code] (default), the mouse wheel scrolls the view vertically, and holding [kbd]Shift[/kbd] scrolls horizontally.

members.name: ScrollHorizontalCustomStep
members.summary: Overrides the [member ScrollBar.custom_step] used when clicking the internal scroll bar's horizontal increment and decrement buttons or when using arrow keys when the [ScrollBar] is focused.

members.name: ScrollVertical
members.summary: The current vertical scroll value. [b]Note:[/b] Setting it early needs to be deferred, just like in [member scroll_horizontal]. [codeblock] func _ready(): set_deferred("scroll_vertical", 600) [/codeblock]

members.name: ScrollVerticalCustomStep
members.summary: Overrides the [member ScrollBar.custom_step] used when clicking the internal scroll bar's vertical increment and decrement buttons or when using arrow keys when the [ScrollBar] is focused.

members.name: TileScrollHint
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the scroll hint texture will be tiled instead of stretched. See [member scroll_hint_mode].

members.name: VerticalScrollMode
members.summary: Controls whether vertical scrollbar can be used and when it should be visible.

signals.name: ScrollEnded
signals.summary: Emitted when scrolling stops when dragging the scrollable area [i]with a touch event[/i]. This signal is [i]not[/i] emitted when scrolling by dragging the scrollbar, scrolling with the mouse wheel or scrolling with keyboard/gamepad events. [b]Note:[/b] This signal is only emitted on Android or iOS, or on desktop/web platforms when [member ProjectSettings.input_devices/pointing/emulate_touch_from_mouse] is enabled.

signals.name: ScrollStarted
signals.summary: Emitted when scrolling starts when dragging the scrollable area [i]with a touch event[/i]. This signal is [i]not[/i] emitted when scrolling by dragging the scrollbar, scrolling with the mouse wheel or scrolling with keyboard/gamepad events. [b]Note:[/b] This signal is only emitted on Android or iOS, or on desktop/web platforms when [member ProjectSettings.input_devices/pointing/emulate_touch_from_mouse] is enabled.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_ALL
values.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top and bottom (if vertical), or left and right (if horizontal).

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTTOM_AND_RIGHT
values.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the bottom (if horizontal), or the right (if horizontal).

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Scroll hints will never be shown.

values.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_TOP_AND_LEFT
values.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top (if vertical), or the left (if horizontal).

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_AUTO
values.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be visible only if necessary, i.e. container's content is bigger than the container.

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_DISABLED
values.summary: Scrolling disabled, scrollbar will be invisible.

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_MAXIMIZE_FIRST
values.summary: Behaves like [constant SCROLL_MODE_AUTO], but makes the [ScrollContainer] report a minimum size based on its content (limited by [member Control.custom_maximum_size] when set on the corresponding axis). This allows it to grow first and only start scrolling once constrained.

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_RESERVE
values.summary: Combines [constant SCROLL_MODE_AUTO] and [constant SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_ALWAYS]. The scrollbar is only visible if necessary, but the content size is adjusted as if it was always visible. It's useful for ensuring that content size stays the same regardless if the scrollbar is visible.

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_ALWAYS
values.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be always visible.

values.name: SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_NEVER
values.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be hidden.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Scrolling disabled, scrollbar will be invisible.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_AUTO
constants.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be visible only if necessary, i.e. container's content is bigger than the container.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_ALWAYS
constants.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be always visible.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_NEVER
constants.summary: Scrolling enabled, scrollbar will be hidden.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_RESERVE
constants.summary: Combines [constant SCROLL_MODE_AUTO] and [constant SCROLL_MODE_SHOW_ALWAYS]. The scrollbar is only visible if necessary, but the content size is adjusted as if it was always visible. It's useful for ensuring that content size stays the same regardless if the scrollbar is visible.

constants.name: SCROLL_MODE_MAXIMIZE_FIRST
constants.summary: Behaves like [constant SCROLL_MODE_AUTO], but makes the [ScrollContainer] report a minimum size based on its content (limited by [member Control.custom_maximum_size] when set on the corresponding axis). This allows it to grow first and only start scrolling once constrained.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_DISABLED
constants.summary: Scroll hints will never be shown.

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_ALL
constants.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top and bottom (if vertical), or left and right (if horizontal).

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_TOP_AND_LEFT
constants.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the top (if vertical), or the left (if horizontal).

constants.name: SCROLL_HINT_MODE_BOTTOM_AND_RIGHT
constants.summary: Scroll hints will be shown at the bottom (if horizontal), or the right (if horizontal).
